title: Chicken and Lentil Stew
description: Bone-in chicken thighs simmered with lentils, tomatoes, and spinach in a cumin-smoked paprika broth. High protein, high fiber, batch-friendly. Great all week.

instructions.text: Dice onions, carrots, and celery. Mince garlic. Remove skin from chicken thighs if not already done. Rinse lentils under cold water.

Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat. Add onions, carrots, and celery and cook until softened, about 6 minutes. Add garlic, cumin, and smoked paprika and stir 1 minute until fragrant.
Nestle chicken thighs into the pot. Pour in broth and add diced tomatoes and bay leaves. Season with salt and pepper. Bring to a boil over high heat, about 5 minutes.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer until chicken is cooked through and tender enough to shred, about 25 minutes.
Remove chicken thighs to a cutting board. Add lentils to the pot, stir, and keep simmering uncovered. Shred chicken with two forks, discarding bones, about 5 minutes.
Continue simmering uncovered until lentils are fully tender, about 20 minutes. Discard bay leaves. The stew will thicken as the lentils absorb liquid.
Return shredded chicken to the pot. Stir in spinach and cook until wilted, about 2 minutes. Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve hot or cool completely and refrigerate up to 5 days.

notes:
Lentils continue absorbing liquid as the stew sits -- add a splash of broth when reheating.

Freezes well for up to 3 months.

If batch-cooking for multiple days, consider keeping spinach out of the pot and adding it fresh per bowl -- it wilts down and gets slimy after a day or two in the fridge.
